_doc: Machine-readable manifest of every HARD / MUST rule in spec/AGENTS*.md (CODEX-CODING-SPEC). Mirrors claudemd/spec/hard-rules.json, adapted for Codex. Drives: (1) the hit-rate promote/demote governance (rules with 0 hits over a review window are always-on-layer dilution → demote to extended; high-hit rules justify core residence), (2) the Phase-1+ hook build (codex_hook_event names the event a hook attaches to), (3) a future CI drift test asserting each section_anchor still exists verbatim in the named spec file. Maintenance: when the spec adds/removes a HARD rule, update this manifest in the SAME edit. enforcement values: self (agent-enforced, no mechanical check) | hook (Codex native hook enforces) | external (offline codex-exec audit / operator) | both. last_demote_review is null until codexmd has deployed telemetry (pre-deployment: no field data exists yet).
_live_sections_doc: spec_section values currently EMITTED by a built+registered hook. scripts/rules.js only treats a hook-enforced rule as a demote-candidate when its section is live (0 hits on a live section = dilution); a hook-enforced rule whose section is NOT live is 'hook-planned' (its hook is not built yet, so 0 hits is expected, not dilution). Update this array in the same commit a hook starts/stops emitting a section.

rules.name: rm -rf $VAR safety (immutable)
rules.note: Immutable §8. First blocking hook to port (pre-bash-safety-check.sh).

rules.name: Unknown-origin script execution (immutable)
rules.note: Includes unpinned npx / curl|bash detection; §5 hard also lists it.

rules.name: No plaintext secrets in code/logs/commits (immutable)
rules.note: Hook scans outgoing git commit/diff for key/token patterns; self-enforced elsewhere.

rules.name: No DELETE/UPDATE/DROP without WHERE (immutable)
rules.note: Reliable mechanical detection across SQL dialects/ORMs is infeasible at the bash-hook layer; self-enforced (claudemd also leaves this self).

rules.name: No unbounded recursive traversal of home/config dirs (immutable)
rules.section_anchor: unbounded recursive traversal of home/config dirs
rules.note: Hook can flag unscoped rg/find/ls -R on ~/.codex ~/.claude ~ without -maxdepth; self-enforced for the general case.

rules.name: Verify-before-claim V1-V4
rules.note: V4 sandbox-artifact disposal partially hookable via a Stop residue/disposal check; V1-V3 self-enforced.

rules.name: Sandbox-artifact disposal (V4)
rules.note: Stop-hook advisory on mkdtemp/scratch residue newer than session baseline.

rules.name: Iron Law #1: no change without pre-change evidence (L2+)
Iron Law #2: no done without fresh evidence
rules.note: Never deferrable (not even under EMERGENCY). Partially observable via Stop transcript-structure scan (evidence-anchor presence).

rules.name: Iron Law #3: no fix without root cause (L2+)
Bugfix anchor + banned phrasings (missing-evidence tokens)
rules.note: Shares the banned-vocab pattern set with §10 Specificity; hook scans commit prose + Stop transcript.

rules.name: Destructive smoke test before live run (§8.V3)
MEMORY.md read-the-file at ship/destructive/L3
rules.section_anchor: MUST read the linked file before proceeding (HARD at ship/destructive/L3)
rules.note: Hook fires on git push/merge/publish Bash; requires a matched MEMORY.md Read earlier in session.

rules.name: Exit archival (HARD) — distill dead-ends to memory/ before abnormal exit
Session-exit mid-SPINE (HARD) — un-validated work is not 'Completed'
Ship-baseline: full validation green + fresh before push
rules.note: §E3 ship-checklist item 2 (the specific green+fresh gate); hook fires on git push to a shared branch. Distinct from §E3-ship-checklist (the full gate order).

rules.name: Four-section REPORT order (Done -> Not done -> Failed -> Uncertain)
rules.note: Stop transcript-structure scan checks section presence + order in four-section reports.

rules.name: Honesty rules (yes/no first, no evaluative framing, no hedging)
Specificity + banned vocab (absolute numbers / baseline-anchored ratios)
rules.note: Hook enforces banned-vocab subset on git commit messages; Stop scan covers assistant prose; self-enforced elsewhere.

rules.name: Plan before execute (L2+, MUST) via update_plan
Recurrence check (L1 bugfix) via git log --grep
rules.note: Git history is the signature store — the recurrence detector runs without extended loaded.

rules.name: Hard AUTH gates (ask, block) — semantic, not replaced by sandbox/approval config
rules.note: Some entries (delete/migration/push) overlap hook-enforced rules; the AUTH judgment itself is self-enforced.

rules.name: Level hard-upgrades (API/auth/payment, migration/infra, released-artifact, LLM-visible metadata)
Preflight git status before edits (L1+)
End-of-task sweep (L2+) — no orphan files
rules.note: Stop residue-audit advisory on ~/.codex/tmp growth; the git-status sweep is self-enforced.

rules.name: L3 phase gate (no phase N+1 while phase N un-validated)
rules.section_anchor: no phase N+1 while phase N is un-validated

rules.name: Ship/release checklist gate order
rules.section_anchor: Gate order — a red item stops the pipeline

rules.name: L3 evidence ladder (claim strength <= tier actually run)
Skill/prompt/MCP-description authoring = LLM-visible metadata = L3
